children.label: A
children.text: to oversee the prudential operations of each regulated entity; and

children.label: i
children.text: each regulated entity operates in a safe and sound manner, including maintenance of adequate capital and internal controls;

children.label: ii
children.text: the operations and activities of each regulated entity foster liquid, efficient, competitive, and resilient national housing finance markets (including activities relating to mortgages on housing for low- and moderate-income families involving a reasonable economic return that may be less than the return earned on other activities);

children.label: iii
children.text: each regulated entity complies with this chapter and the rules, regulations, guidelines, and orders issued under this chapter and the authorizing statutes;

children.label: iv
children.text: each regulated entity carries out its statutory mission only through activities that are authorized under and consistent with this chapter and the authorizing statutes; and

children.label: v
children.text: the activities of each regulated entity and the manner in which such regulated entity is operated are consistent with the public interest.

children.label: A
children.text: to review and, if warranted based on the principal duties described in paragraph (1), reject any acquisition or transfer of a controlling interest in a regulated entity; and

children.label: B
children.text: to exercise such incidental powers as may be necessary or appropriate to fulfill the duties and responsibilities of the Director in the supervision and regulation of each regulated entity.

children.heading: Coordination with the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
children.label: 3 Coordination with the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: A Consultation
children.text: The Director shall consult with, and consider the views of, the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, with respect to the risks posed by the regulated entities to the financial system, prior to issuing any proposed or final regulations, orders, and guidelines with respect to the exercise of the additional authority provided in this Act regarding prudential management and operations standards, safe and sound operations of, and capital requirements and portfolio standards applicable to the regulated entities (as such term is defined in ). The Director also shall consult with the Chairman regarding any decision to place a regulated entity into conservatorship or receivership.section 4502 of this title

children.heading: Information sharing
children.label: B Information sharing
children.text: To facilitate the consultative process, the Director shall share information with the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System on a regular, periodic basis as determined by the Director and the Board regarding the capital, asset and liabilities, financial condition, and risk management practices of the regulated entities as well as any information related to financial market stability.

children.heading: Termination of consultation requirement
children.label: C Termination of consultation requirement
children.text: The requirement of the Director to consult with the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System under this paragraph shall expire at the conclusion of .December 31, 2009

children.heading: Delegation of authority
children.label: b Delegation of authority
children.text: The Director may delegate to officers and employees of the Agency any of the functions, powers, or duties of the Director, as the Director considers appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In enforcing any provision of this chapter, any regulation or order prescribed under this chapter, or any other provision of law, rule, regulation, or order, or in any other action, suit, or proceeding to which the Director is a party or in which the Director is interested, and in the administration of conservatorships and receiverships, the Director may act in the Director’s own name and through the Director’s own attorneys.

children.heading: Subject to suit
children.label: 2 Subject to suit
children.text: Except as otherwise provided by law, the Director shall be subject to suit (other than suits on claims for money damages) by a regulated entity with respect to any matter under this chapter or any other applicable provision of law, rule, order, or regulation under this chapter, in the United States district court for the judicial district in which the regulated entity has its principal place of business, or in the United States District Court for the District of Columbia, and the Director may be served with process in the manner prescribed by the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

children.heading: Recognition of distinctions between the enterprises and the Federal Home Loan Banks1So in original. No subsecs. (d) and (e) have been enacted.1
children.label: f Recognition of distinctions between the enterprises and the Federal Home Loan Banks1So in original. No subsecs. (d) and (e) have been enacted.1

children.text: cooperative ownership structure;

children.label: B
children.text: the mission of providing liquidity to members;2So in original. The word “the” probably should not appear.2

children.label: C
children.text: affordable housing and community development mission;

children.label: D
children.text: capital structure; and

children.label: E
children.text: joint and several liability; and
any other differences that the Director considers appropriate.
